description_long: This is a classic cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top and a weathered, red-painted exterior, showing signs of rust and wear. The hydrant features two side outlets and one front outlet, each capped and secured with metal chains to prevent loss of the caps. The top dome is ribbed and metallic, with a central operating nut for valve control. The base is wide and circular, designed for stability when bolted to the ground. The surface is predominantly red with patches of exposed metal and grime, indicating long-term outdoor exposure. Faint markings and graffiti are visible on the body, adding to its urban character.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with a domed top, three capped outlets, and metal chains securing the caps. The surface is rusted and marked, showing signs of long-term outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main cylindrical body, domed top, two side outlets, and a front outlet, all with attached chains and visible surface wear.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting one side outlet, the attached chain, and the cylindrical form with visible rust and grime.
description_view_2: Direct view of the front outlet and both side outlets, with all chains and caps clearly visible, emphasizing the symmetrical design.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other side outlet, chain, and the weathered surface details.
